campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met on May 19, 2026, to discuss staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also heard public comments on various matters and noted that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Public hearing on government code section 3502 matters
Boogie Festival happening this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council approved an engineer's report and moved forward with a formal process for a new ordinance, scheduling it for final adoption on May 20. The council also discussed whether Saratoga should become a charter city and reviewed preliminary tax and revenue options.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report preliminary approval for fiscal year
New ordinance scheduled for adoption vote on May 20
Potential transition to charter city status
Preliminary tax and revenue options under consideration

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 16, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 16, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in June and authorize the Town Manager to sign several contract agreements. The contracts covered software services, legal representation, and litigation support for the town.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from June 2 and June 8
Authorized new five-year software services agreement
Authorized amendments to existing legal service contracts
Authorized litigation legal services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on June 23, 2026, to discuss several major items including protecting a historic building at 647 South Sixth Street, changing how the city elects mayors and council members to use ranked choice voting, approving advertising rights at the airport, and signing a long-term power purchase agreement with an energy company.
keyTopics: Historic building protection and Mills Act tax contract for 647 South Sixth Street property
Ranked choice voting option for filling mayor and councilmember vacancies
Large format advertising sign contract at San José Airport
Long-term energy supply agreement with Shell Energy North America

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to address budget planning for the next fiscal year, approve water management plans, and recognize community milestones including City Attorney Jennifer Logue's appointment to the judiciary. The council also discussed two pending legal cases and various city infrastructure projects including a new public safety building and shoreline restoration work.
keyTopics: Two anticipated lawsuits facing the city requiring legal counsel discussion
Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget and spending limits
5-year water management plan update and drought contingency measures
Public Safety Building construction project
South Bay Salt Pond restoration and shoreline golf course parking lot improvements

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular public council meeting at 7 P.M. The closed session portion addressed confidential matters that weren't detailed in the public agenda.
keyTopics: Closed-session meeting held before regular council session
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.
Specific agenda items for public session not detailed in this summary

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a study session to review and update Cupertino's Health and Safety Element, which is part of the city's long-range planning document through 2040. The council also recognized the Parks and Recreation Department for their work across multiple divisions and celebrated Monta Vista High School's Science Olympiad Team achievement.
keyTopics: Update to Health and Safety Element of General Plan (2015-2040)
Recognition of Parks and Recreation Department divisions and programs
Monta Vista High School Science Olympiad Team achievement

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a hybrid meeting on June 30, 2026, allowing residents to attend in person or join remotely via Zoom. The city is continuing its practice of accepting written public comments from residents before meetings through their online system.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation options for residents
Written public comment submission process via eComments

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to discuss updates to the city's five-year housing and community development plan, review requests from council members for new work projects, and consider changes to how the city fills vacant council seats. The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan for housing and community development funding
Changes to the Council Vacancy Ordinance to reduce certain procedures
New workplan requests submitted by individual council members
Upcoming city council meeting schedule and agenda preview

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 10, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to handle routine administrative matters and approve a change to building setback requirements at 3300 El Camino Real. The main action item was updating the required distance (setback) that buildings must be set back from Hansen Way from its current requirement to a new 20-foot setback.
keyTopics: Updated building setback requirement at 3300 El Camino Real (Hansen Way) changed to 20 feet
Approval of Planning & Transportation Commission meeting minutes from April 29, 2026
City Council director's report, meeting schedule, and work assignments
